summary: We are in a critical execution phase for the China Going-Global report and Asia Start campaign, while simultaneously pushing forward the knowledge website redesign and SEO prioritization, with this week's focus on finalizing report structure, launching dual campaign routes, and completing website feedback.
summaryDetail: The top priority is the China Going-Global report (working title 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'), a joint CKGSB-IE report on Chinese companies expanding to Southeast Asia, targeting a digital release by early September for Singapore events at Tech in Asia or Tyson. This week, Felix must deliver a one-pager proposal on structure and promotion, and the team needs to start translating the Chinese report and identifying alumni case studies. Simultaneously, the Asia Start campaign launches with two parallel routes (AI-driven by Joseph and traditional by Daisy) to test messaging, while the new Intelligent China program website needs completion and SCM campaign planning to differentiate it as a deeper dive. On the knowledge website redesign, Joseph and Julie need to mark feedback on Joker's proposal and share Joseph's HTML alternative, with a decision to keep the two-menu structure and incorporate indices more prominently. Urgent this week: finalize the Shun Tian Shu press release approach (single release or 10-point article), provide feedback on the website menu, and follow up with Media Minds as the prioritized social media agency. Upcoming: the CKGSB-EuroCham EV event requires PO submission and key message review, and the Mei Jianping Art Indices report needs a news release and media distribution.

meetings.title: Knowledge Website Redesign Review
meetings.summary: Team reviewed Joker's redesign proposal for the knowledge website, noting the menu categorization improvement but overall similarity to current design. They discussed incorporating elements from Joseph's alternative layout to show more articles and indices.

meetings.title: SEO & Program Prioritization Strategy
meetings.summary: Team discusses SEO strategy to improve search rankings for programs like Asia Star and Intelligent China, prioritizing these two programs over US partnerships. They plan to audit existing conversion tracking and analyze target audience profiles.

meetings.title: Marketing Team Meeting - June 29, 2026
meetings.summary: Team discussed website menu feedback, brochure updates, social media agency search, and IE report translation. Decisions made on Shun Tian Shu press release approach and prioritizing Media Minds for social media.

meetings.title: New Chinese Playbook Report Planning
meetings.summary: Team discusses joint report between CKGSB and IE on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, with op-eds from deans and case studies. Report to launch at Tech in Asia or Tyson event in Singapore in mid-September.

meetings.title: Asia Start & New Program Campaign Planning
meetings.summary: Discussed campaign overview for Asia Start, with two routes (AI by Joseph, traditional by Daisy) for first month, and introduced a new 'Intelligent China' program with modules in Beijing, Shanghai, Hangzhou, and Shenzhen.

meetings.title: EU-China Cooperation Discussion
meetings.summary: Discussion on EU-China relations, focusing on geopolitical tensions, de-risking, and the role of companies versus governments in shaping cooperation.

decisions.text: Remove dedicated video section; videos will be dispersed by topic.
Keep the two-menu structure (knowledge menu and school menu).
Incorporate indices (BCI, BSI) more prominently on the knowledge homepage.
Prioritize Asia Star and Intelligent China programs over US partnerships for initial SEO efforts.
Include Berkeley program as a third priority if resources allow.
Only one press release for Shun Tian Shu, either incorporating 10 points or converting to 10-point article.
Prioritize Media Minds as social media agency due to small budget and time constraints.
Report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE, with main research from Professor Leeway and op-eds from deans and possibly alumni.
Working title: 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'.
Target to have digital version ready by early September for Singapore events.
Asia Start will run two parallel campaigns for the first month: AI-driven by Joseph and traditional by Daisy.
decisions.meetingTitle: Asia Start & New Program Campaign Planning

decisions.text: Intelligent China program will be marketed as a deeper dive compared to Asia Start to avoid cannibalization.
decisions.meetingTitle: Asia Start & New Program Campaign Planning
